root_motivation: Skill text (~/.claude/skills/*/SKILL.md) drifts from installed tool surfaces; four live drift instances already observed. The user wants automated detection that produces review-gated proposals rather than auto-edits, mirroring the recall observe pattern. Detection must be decoupled from application so the same checker can feed multiple downstream consumers later.
user_persona: Claude (this agent) and the user (jsy) reviewing the proposal queue at ~/.claude/skill-doctor/proposals/*.md. Same review idiom as recall observe and freshness/recall-doctor-claims.

unfakeable_metric.name: ac_passing_count
unfakeable_metric.harness_command: cargo test --release --quiet 2>&1 | grep -E '^test result' | awk '{s+=$4} END {print s}'

acceptance_criteria.description: cargo build --release green on fresh clone; cargo test --release --lib green.
skill-doctor check against live ~/.claude/skills/ produces >=4 drift proposals matching the four known instances.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac2.rs (run check against fixture mirroring the live drift cases)

acceptance_criteria.description: Proposals written under ~/.claude/skill-doctor/proposals/<ULID>.md with the schema in PRD section Proposal output.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac3.rs (parse generated proposal frontmatter + body shape)

acceptance_criteria.description: Re-running skill-doctor check does not create duplicate proposals for the same (skill_path, line, binary, drift_kind, detail) tuple.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac4.rs (run check twice, assert proposal count unchanged on second)

acceptance_criteria.description: skill-doctor proposals reject <ULID> flips status: rejected; proposal disappears from list output.
Missing tool-manifest handled gracefully: prints friendly error and exits 2 when ~/.claude/tool-manifest/manifest.json absent.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac6.rs (env override manifest path to nonexistent file; assert exit 2 + stderr)

acceptance_criteria.description: False-positive rate <=30% on the live skill set: >=70% of generated proposals reflect actual drift.
acceptance_criteria.test: Manual review of first batch; documented in postmortem (not gated by cargo test)

acceptance_criteria.description: Proposal queue path created with mode 0700.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac8.rs (stat the directory after first run; assert mode & 0777 == 0o700)

acceptance_criteria.description: Crate published to github.com/j0yen/skill-doctor under dual MIT+Apache-2.0 license with README citing drift vision and tool-manifest dependency.
acceptance_criteria.test: Stage 6 publish step + LICENSE-MIT + LICENSE-APACHE files present

acceptance_criteria.description: ~/.local/bin/skill-doctor installed via bootstrap/install.sh; row added to ~/wintermute/REPOS.md.
Verified-completed: user reviews first batch, runs proposals promote, applies a recommended edit by hand, confirms drift gone.

scope: Extract shell invocations from fenced bash/sh/shell blocks and backtick inline code
Cross-reference each invocation's binary/subcommand/flags against tool-manifest manifest.json
Park drift findings as proposal files at ~/.claude/skill-doctor/proposals/<ULID>.md
Dedupe pending proposals by content hash to make repeated runs idempotent
CLI surface: check, proposals list, proposals show, proposals reject, proposals promote
non_goals: Auto-edit of skill files (Fleet 2 candidate drift-skill-doctor-fix)
Parsing multiline backslash-continuations, heredocs, or shell-variable-expanded commands
Auto-syncing the manifest in check (consumers must call tool-manifest sync explicitly)

five_whys_trace.a: Skill text drifts from installed tool surfaces and the user keeps spotting it manually.
Skills are markdown maintained by humans (and Claude); installed binaries evolve independently. No automated cross-check.

five_whys_trace.q: Why a proposal queue instead of auto-edit?
five_whys_trace.a: False positives are inevitable when extracting shell from prose; review-gated changes mirror the recall observe pattern the user is already trained on.

five_whys_trace.q: Why must detection be decoupled from application?
five_whys_trace.a: So the same checker can feed multiple downstream consumers (drift-proposals skill, self-review Phase A integration) without re-implementing extraction.

five_whys_trace.q: Why tool-manifest as the ground truth (not parsing live --help)?
five_whys_trace.a: tool-manifest already ships, has the JSON format we need, supports query/show. Re-implementing --help probing here would couple skill-doctor to runtime binary presence and double the surface area to test.

ambiguities_resolved.question: Where do proposal files live?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: ~/.claude/skill-doctor/proposals/<ULID>.md (per PRD); mode 0700 per AC8 + recall observe convention.

ambiguities_resolved.question: How to identify candidate shell commands inside markdown prose?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Fenced bash/sh/shell blocks; backtick inline code starting with a known binary name (binary names sourced from tool-manifest).
